message:
Add web/ — Vite docs site with live in-browser bench

Two-page static site styled to match ../turboquant-wasm/demo:

  /              landing — overview, headline numbers vs capnweb,
                 demo cards linking to playground + honest comparison
  /playground    live bench — REST/JSON vs capnwasm side-by-side,
                 fetches 100 user records in parallel, decodes,
                 renders to DOM, reports phase-by-phase timing

Setup:
  cd web
  npm install
  npm run dev       # vite at :5173
  npm run build     # static output in web/dist

The playground is intentionally honest. For 100 small records on
localhost the bench shows capnwasm ~1.04× SLOWER than REST/JSON —
the wasm boundary cost on per-field reads outweighs JSON.parse for
small inputs. The summary text says so explicitly. The wins
capnwasm offers (bigger payloads, binary data, RPC bursts) need a
different workload to surface; that's a follow-up bench.

Generated artifacts (web/public/data/*, web/public/capnp.slim.wasm)
are gitignored — `npm run prepare` regenerates them from the
shipped runtime + a small fixture script.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,95 @@ + + + + + + capnwasm — Cap'n Proto for the browser + + + + + + +
+

capnwasm

+

Real Cap'n Proto C++ in the browser. Typed RPC, binary wire, 44 KB gzip.

+

+ One typed-client toolchain for any backend — write a .capnp schema, get + a fast typed JS client back. npx capnwasm gen user.capnp emits a reader/builder + that runs against real upstream Cap'n Proto C++ statically compiled to wasm. +

+ +
+

vs capnweb (in-process bench, Apple Silicon)

+
+ Burst 1000 calls (per-call) + 2.5 μs — 3.2x faster +
+
+ 64 KB text echo round-trip + 96 μs — 3.8x faster +
+
+ Single tiny RPC call + 8.5 μs — 1.75x faster +
+
+ Bundle (JS + wasm, gzip) + 44 KB — 2.1x larger +
+
+ Cold start (init + first call) + ~3 ms — 15x slower +
+
+ +
+ +
⚡
+
Live playground
+
+ Run REST/JSON, capnweb, and capnwasm side-by-side in your browser against the + same backend. See the wire bytes, decode time, and render-to-DOM time for each. +
+ Interactive + + + +
⚖️
+
Honest comparison
+
+ Where capnwasm wins, where it loses, where it’s tied. With reproducible + numbers, no marketing handwave. Bundle size and cold start go to capnweb. +
+ Tradeoffs + + + +
🚀
+
Quickstart
+
+ .capnp schemas, TypeScript interfaces with @rest directives, + or OpenAPI specs → typed JS client. npx capnwasm gen. +
+ 3 quickstarts + + + +
🔗
+
RPC pillars
+
+ Promise pipelining, capability passing, auto-release via FinalizationRegistry, + microtask batching, streaming. Wire-compatible with C++/Rust/Go peers. +
+ Full RPC + +
+ +
+ npm + GitHub + Playground +
+
+ +

@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+import { defineConfig } from "vite";
+import { resolve } from "node:path";
+
+// Multi-page Vite site: a landing page and the live perf playground.
+// Pages are wired up in rollupOptions.input so the build emits both.
+export default defineConfig(({ command }) => ({
+  // For local dev / playwright tests we serve from "/". For a published
+  // GitHub Pages deploy, set base to "/capnwasm/" via env or a CI flag.
+  base: "/",
+  build: {
+    outDir: "dist",
+    emptyOutDir: true,
+    target: "esnext",
+    rollupOptions: {
+      input: {
+        index: resolve(__dirname, "index.html"),
+        playground: resolve(__dirname, "playground.html"),
+      },
+    },
+  },
+  server: {
+    port: 5173,
+    fs: {
+      // Allow Vite to serve files from the parent capnwasm dir so the
+      // playground can `import "../../js/rpc.mjs"` directly during dev.
+      // Production builds resolve everything through the bundler instead.
+      allow: [resolve(__dirname, ".."), __dirname],
+    },
+  },
+}));
